Disturbance–response analysis II — wavelet synchrony (coherence, cross-power, phase → apparent lag, amplitude)

Robust signal-processing pass: Morlet wavelet at the P≈500 band, pre vs post — the native wavelet view, read against analysis I’s moments + peaks

nic (nic@cascadeclimate.org)

2026-07-17

Analysis I described synchrony with moments + peak spacings over frozen pre/post windows — the simplest thing that works, but peak-finding frays on noisy/irregular signals and its lag is a coarse few-sample offset. This pass takes the native wavelet view: the Morlet wavelet (via WaveletComp) resolves synchrony jointly in time and frequency, so we can isolate the generator’s P≈500 band and watch coupling evolve through the disturbance, not just at the settled ends. Deliberately different metrics from analysis I — the point is to compare what each approach sees (there is no attempt to reproduce the peak-based numbers).

Four band-localized quantities, all read at P≈500 (the driver’s period):

  • Wavelet coherence ∈[0,1] — normalized (smoothed) coupling strength.
  • Cross-wavelet power — strength of shared oscillation at the band (un-normalized).
  • Phase difference φ (radians) — Angle = phase.climate − phase.response, the raw synchrony phase; climate leads ⇒ positive φ. Shown in the tables alongside its step-unit form:
  • Apparent lag = φ · P / (2π) steps — φ rescaled to the interpretable “how many steps behind” (the figures use this; the tables carry both).
  • Amplitude ratio — physical band amplitude response / climate (see the ×sd note in band_trace: WaveletComp standardizes each channel, so the raw amplitude ratio must be rescaled back to physical units).

Two caveats carried throughout. (1) COI/edge: at period P the Morlet spans ~±707 steps, so band metrics are only trustworthy ≥707 steps from a series end and from a regime boundary — settled averages use those buffered windows, and trace edges are blanked. (2) Uncertainty: the band series is heavily autocorrelated, so error bars use a block SE — one estimate per P-long cycle, SE = dispersion/√n_blocks (a circular dispersion for the phase, since linear sd on wrapped angles is invalid). The COI buffer leaves only ~2 cycles per settled window (reported as n_blocks), so these SEs carry ~1 degree of freedom: read them as coarse guides to whether an effect clears its noise, not precise intervals. Shown next to each pre→post change so effect size is read against its uncertainty.

Helpers

# ---- small numerics -------------------------------------------------------------------------
smooth_ma <- function(x, w = 51) as.numeric(stats::filter(x, rep(1 / w, w)))  # centered MA; NA at ±(w-1)/2 ends
CMEAN     <- function(a) Arg(mean(exp(1i * a)))          # circular mean of angles (phase never averaged linearly)
band_rows <- function(wc) which(abs(wc$Period - P) <= 0.05 * P)  # the few scale-rows within ±5% of P

# wc.image styling is identical across all three scenario spectra — only the object + title vary.
# color.key = "interval": linear 0→1 colour scale. ("quantile" warps it — coherence saturates
# near 1, so quantile breaks bunch the legend into a 0/0.9/1 mess.)
plot_wc <- function(wc, main)
  wc.image(wc, which.image = "wc", plot.arrow = TRUE, plot.contour = FALSE,
           color.key = "interval", timelab = "time", periodlab = "period",
           legend.params = list(lab = "wavelet coherence"), main = main)

# ---- WaveletComp wrapper --------------------------------------------------------------------
# loess.span = 0: no detrend (series are trend-free) ⇒ standardization is exactly /sd(x), which
#   makes the ×sd amplitude rescale in band_trace exact.
# my.pair = climate,response ⇒ Angle = phase.climate − phase.response, so climate-leads is +.
# make.pval = FALSE: skip significance surrogates — a valid 5% contour needs ≥100 (~30-50 min),
#   and coherence saturates here so it would mislead. Uncertainty = COI + block SEs instead.
# lowerPeriod/upperPeriod bracket P by ±1.5 octaves so the coupling ridge sits mid-axis in the
#   coherence image (upper ≈ the series length limit, above which everything is COI). This also
#   anchors the scale grid ~onto P — band metrics shift only in the last digit vs a [2,1024] grid.
wco <- function(df)
  analyze.coherency(as.data.frame(df[, c("climate", "response")]),
                    my.pair = c("climate", "response"), loess.span = 0,
                    dt = 1, dj = 1/20, lowerPeriod = P / 2^1.5, upperPeriod = P * 2^1.5,
                    make.pval = FALSE, verbose = FALSE)

# ---- collapse the wc object to one time-resolved series at the P band ------------------------
band_trace <- function(wc, df) {
  bi   <- band_rows(wc)
  sd_c <- sd(df$climate); sd_r <- sd(df$response)          # the constants WaveletComp divided out (loess.span=0)
  ang  <- apply(wc$Angle[bi, , drop = FALSE], 2, CMEAN)    # per-time band phase difference
  tibble(time      = df$time,
         phase     = df$phase,
         coherence = colMeans(wc$Coherence[bi, , drop = FALSE]),
         xpower    = colMeans(wc$Power.xy[bi, , drop = FALSE]),                 # shared-oscillation power at band
         amp_ratio = colMeans(wc$Ampl.y[bi, , drop = FALSE]) /
                     colMeans(wc$Ampl.x[bi, , drop = FALSE]) * (sd_r / sd_c),   # ×sd: undo per-channel standardization → physical ratio
         angle     = ang,
         lag       = ang / (2 * pi) * P,                                        # phase → steps
         coi_ok    = df$time >= EF & df$time <= nrow(df) - EF)                  # trustworthy (outside COI) mask
}

# ---- settled pre/post summary with block SEs ------------------------------------------------
# One estimate per P-long cycle in a COI-buffered window, then value ± dispersion/√n_blocks.
# Valid under autocorrelation (a per-step SE would be far too narrow). `circ` uses a circular
# dispersion — linear sd on wrapped angles is invalid (phases straddling ±π look far apart).
# The COI buffer leaves only ~2 cycles per window, so n is tiny — SEs are coarse (see intro).
block_stat <- function(t, v, circ = FALSE) {
  b  <- (t - min(t)) %/% P
  bv <- as.numeric(tapply(seq_along(t), b, function(i) if (circ) CMEAN(v[i]) else mean(v[i])))
  n  <- length(bv)
  if (circ) {
    R    <- Mod(mean(exp(1i * bv)))                       # resultant length of block phases (1 = locked, 0 = scattered)
    disp <- if (R < 1) sqrt(-2 * log(R)) else 0           # circular sd
  } else disp <- sd(bv)
  list(value = if (circ) CMEAN(bv) else mean(bv),
       se    = if (n > 1) disp / sqrt(n) else NA_real_, n = n)
}

# Regime boundaries come from the `phase` column itself — one source of truth, so the settled
# windows can't silently desync from the data (the disturbance step and transition span differ
# by scenario; Return's transition runs to 2500, the others to 2250).
wave_summary <- function(tr) {
  td     <- tr$time[tr$phase == "disturbance"]            # the single disturbance step
  tr_end <- max(tr$time[tr$phase == "transition"])        # last step of the transition span
  N      <- max(tr$time)
  wins <- list(pre  = tr$time >= EF          & tr$time <= td - EF,      # ≥ EF from series start AND from disturbance
               post = tr$time >= tr_end + EF & tr$time <= N  - EF)      # ≥ EF from transition end AND from series end
  one <- function(sel) {
    t  <- tr$time[sel]
    co <- block_stat(t, tr$coherence[sel]); xp <- block_stat(t, tr$xpower[sel])
    ar <- block_stat(t, tr$amp_ratio[sel]); an <- block_stat(t, tr$angle[sel], circ = TRUE)
    tibble(n_blocks  = co$n,                                            # cycles the SEs rest on (expect ~2)
           coherence = co$value, coherence_se = co$se,
           xpower    = xp$value, xpower_se    = xp$se,
           amp_ratio = ar$value, amp_ratio_se = ar$se,
           phi       = an$value,                 phi_se = an$se,          # phase difference φ, radians (circular SE)
           lag       = an$value / (2*pi) * P, lag_se = an$se / (2*pi) * P)  # same φ in step units
  }
  bind_rows(pre = one(wins$pre), post = one(wins$post), .id = "phase") %>%
    mutate(phase = factor(phase, c("pre", "post")))
}

# ---- model-based residual (whole series) ----------------------------------------------------
# climate is the exogenous driver, response the dependent variable, so the residual is
# response − predicted, where predicted = gain · climate(t − lag). lag & gain are fit on the
# `pre` window only (cross-correlation peak + no-intercept OLS), NOT read from the generator,
# so the residual sits at ~0 noise through pre and departs exactly where coupling changes.
fit_pre <- function(df) {
  pre <- df[df$phase == "pre", ]; cl <- pre$climate; rs <- pre$response; n <- length(rs)
  Ls  <- 0:round(0.6 * P)                                              # physically plausible lags only (< ~0.6 period)
  cr  <- vapply(Ls, function(L) cor(rs[(L + 1):n], cl[seq_len(n - L)]), numeric(1))  # cor(response(t), climate(t−L))
  L   <- Ls[which.max(cr)]
  x   <- cl[seq_len(n - L)]; y <- rs[(L + 1):n]
  list(lag = L, gain = sum(x * y) / sum(x * x))                        # OLS slope, no intercept (channels ~zero-mean)
}

resid_trace <- function(df) {
  fp <- fit_pre(df); L <- fp$lag; N <- nrow(df)
  pred <- rep(NA_real_, N); if (L < N) pred[(L + 1):N] <- fp$gain * df$climate[seq_len(N - L)]
  resid <- df$response - pred
  out <- tibble(time = df$time, phase = df$phase,
                resid  = resid,
                dresid = c(NA, diff(smooth_ma(resid, 51))))            # smooth THEN diff so d/dt isn't pure noise
  attr(out, "lag") <- L; attr(out, "gain") <- fp$gain; out
}

# ---- shared plotting furniture (band shading matches analysis I) ----------------------------
phase_fill <- c(pre = "#9ecae1", disturbance = "grey45", transition = "#fcbba1", post = "#fdae6b")
bar_fill   <- phase_fill[c("pre", "post")]                              # the bar plots reuse the pre/post band colours
phase_bands <- function(ph) ph %>% filter(phase != "disturbance") %>%   # disturbance is one step → drawn as a line
  group_by(phase) %>% summarise(xmin = min(time), xmax = max(time), .groups = "drop")

# shared band shading: rect per phase + dashed disturbance line + fill scale.
phase_band_layers <- function(d) {
  bands <- phase_bands(d); td <- d$time[d$phase == "disturbance"]
  list(geom_rect(data = bands, aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = phase),
                 alpha = 0.30, inherit.aes = FALSE),
       geom_vline(xintercept = td, linetype = 2, colour = "grey30"),
       scale_fill_manual(values = phase_fill, guide = "none"))
}

# whole-series residual + its smoothed derivative on a secondary axis, phases shaded AND labelled.
plot_resid <- function(df, title) {
  rt <- resid_trace(df); L <- attr(rt, "lag"); g <- attr(rt, "gain")
  bands <- phase_bands(df)                                             # kept for the phase labels below
  ytop <- max(abs(rt$resid), na.rm = TRUE)
  sc   <- ytop / max(abs(rt$dresid), na.rm = TRUE)                     # scale d/dt onto the residual axis (2nd axis un-scales it)
  ggplot() +
    phase_band_layers(df) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_line(data = rt, aes(time, dresid * sc), colour = "#d94801", linewidth = 0.3, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_line(data = rt, aes(time, resid),       colour = "#238b45", linewidth = 0.4, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_text(data = mutate(bands, x = (xmin + xmax) / 2),
              aes(x = x, y = ytop, label = phase), vjust = 1, size = 3, colour = "grey25") +
    scale_y_continuous(sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / sc, name = "d(residual)/dt  (smoothed, orange)")) +
    labs(title = title, x = "time", y = "residual = response − predicted  (green)",
         subtitle = sprintf("pre-fit predictor:  response(t) ≈ %.2f · climate(t − %d)", g, L))
}

# time-resolved band metrics; COI-contaminated edges blanked; phases shaded.
metric_labs <- c(coherence = "coherence", xpower = "cross power", amp_ratio = "amp ratio", lag = "app. lag")
plot_band_trace <- function(tr, title) {
  m <- names(metric_labs)                                              # the four plotted metrics (single source)
  long <- tr %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(m), ~ ifelse(coi_ok, ., NA_real_))) %>%
    select(time, all_of(m)) %>%
    pivot_longer(-time, names_to = "metric") %>%
    mutate(metric = factor(metric, m))
  ggplot() +
    phase_band_layers(tr) +
    geom_line(data = long, aes(time, value), linewidth = 0.35, na.rm = TRUE) +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1, strip.position = "left",
               labeller = as_labeller(metric_labs)) +
    labs(title = title, x = "time", y = NULL)
}

# reshape a summary tibble to long (id keys × metric × value × se), driven by metric_labs +
# the regular `_se` naming — the one place the value↔se pairing lives. Used by the bars and summary.
metrics_long <- function(sm, keys) {
  m <- names(metric_labs)
  val <- sm %>% select(all_of(c(keys, m))) %>%
    pivot_longer(all_of(m), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")
  se  <- sm %>% select(all_of(c(keys, paste0(m, "_se")))) %>%
    rename_with(~ sub("_se$", "", .x), all_of(paste0(m, "_se"))) %>%
    pivot_longer(all_of(m), names_to = "metric", values_to = "se")
  left_join(val, se, by = c(keys, "metric")) %>% mutate(metric = factor(metric, m))
}

# pre/post bars per metric, ±1 SE (block SE) so effect size is read against uncertainty.
plot_wave_bars <- function(sm, title) {
  dat <- metrics_long(sm, "phase")
  ggplot(dat, aes(phase, value, fill = phase)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = value - se, ymax = value + se), width = 0.25, colour = "grey25", na.rm = TRUE) +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1, labeller = as_labeller(metric_labs)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = bar_fill, guide = "none") +
    labs(title = title, x = NULL, y = NULL)
}

# the settled pre/post table — same columns every scenario (adds n_blocks + phi, no _se).
emit_table <- function(sm, caption)
  cat(kable(sm %>% select(phase, n_blocks, coherence, xpower, amp_ratio, phi, lag),
            digits = 2, caption = caption), sep = "\n")
d_shift  <- read_csv("synthetic_shift.csv",  show_col_types = FALSE)
d_desync <- read_csv("synthetic_desync.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
d_return <- read_csv("synthetic_return.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
# One coherency transform per dataset → reused by every image, trace and table below.
wc_shift  <- wco(d_shift);  tr_shift  <- band_trace(wc_shift,  d_shift);  sm_shift  <- wave_summary(tr_shift)
wc_desync <- wco(d_desync); tr_desync <- band_trace(wc_desync, d_desync); sm_desync <- wave_summary(tr_desync)
wc_return <- wco(d_return); tr_return <- band_trace(wc_return, d_return); sm_return <- wave_summary(tr_return)

Shift

Expectation: coupling survives the disturbance but the regime changes — response amplitude halves (Ar 1.0→0.5) and lag shortens (50→10). Wavelet reading: coherence and cross-power stay high; apparent lag drops ~50→10 and amplitude ratio drops ~1.0→0.5.

plot_resid(d_shift, "Shift — model residual & its derivative")

Figure 1. Shift — model residual (response − pre-fit prediction, green) over the whole series with its smoothed derivative (orange, right axis); phases shaded and labelled, dashed = disturbance. Flat ~0 through pre by construction; a new nonzero regime after the disturbance. 

plot_wc(wc_shift, "Shift — wavelet coherence")

Figure 2. Shift — wavelet coherence spectrum (time × period). Arrows = phase (right = in-phase, down = climate leads); the pale COI parabola marks edge-unreliable regions. Coupling holds across the disturbance at the P≈500 band. 

plot_band_trace(tr_shift, "Shift — synchrony at the P≈500 band, through time")

Figure 3. Shift — band (P≈500) metrics through time; COI edges blanked, phases shaded. Coherence & cross-power hold; apparent lag steps 50→10 and amplitude ratio 1.0→0.5 across the transition. 

emit_table(sm_shift, "Shift — band synchrony, settled pre vs post (n_blocks = cycles the SEs rest on; SEs as error bars)")
Shift — band synchrony, settled pre vs post (n_blocks = cycles the SEs rest on; SEs as error bars)
phase n_blocks coherence xpower amp_ratio phi lag
pre 2 1 2.08 0.99 0.62 49.64
post 2 1 1.03 0.51 0.13 10.67 
plot_wave_bars(sm_shift, "Shift — band metrics, pre vs post (±1 SE)")

Figure 4. Shift — pre vs post band metrics, ±1 SE (block SE). The amp-ratio halving and lag shortening dwarf their error bars; coherence/cross-power barely move — a coherent regime shift.

Read-off: amplitude ratio ≈ 1.0 → 0.5 and apparent lag ≈ 50 → 10 while coherence and cross-power stay high — the wavelet agrees with analysis I’s “new regime, still coupled,” and pins the lag change far more sharply than peak offsets could.

Desync

Expectation: post response is stationary noise matched to regime-n variance — so its energy leaves the P band and spreads across all scales. Wavelet reading: cross-power and amplitude ratio at the band collapse; note whether the normalized coherence follows.

plot_resid(d_desync, "Desync — model residual & its derivative")

Figure 5. Desync — model residual (green) + derivative (orange, right axis), whole series; phases shaded, dashed = disturbance. Residual is ~0 noise in pre, then becomes large and erratic as the response decouples into noise. 

plot_wc(wc_desync, "Desync — wavelet coherence")

Figure 6. Desync — wavelet coherence spectrum (arrows = phase; pale COI parabola = edge-unreliable). Watch the P≈500 band after the disturbance: the smoothed coherence stays high (WaveletComp’s smoothing is too narrow relative to scale, so coherence saturates toward 1 even over noise), so coherence alone does not flag the decoupling — cross-power does. 

plot_band_trace(tr_desync, "Desync — synchrony at the P≈500 band, through time")

Figure 7. Desync — band metrics through time. Cross-power and amplitude ratio crash toward 0 after the disturbance while coherence stays high — the decoupling shows in POWER, not coherence. 

emit_table(sm_desync, "Desync — band synchrony, settled pre vs post (n_blocks = cycles the SEs rest on)")
Desync — band synchrony, settled pre vs post (n_blocks = cycles the SEs rest on)
phase n_blocks coherence xpower amp_ratio phi lag
pre 2 1.00 1.66 0.98 0.63 50.00
post 2 0.99 0.07 0.04 0.37 29.39 
plot_wave_bars(sm_desync, "Desync — band metrics, pre vs post (±1 SE)")

Figure 8. Desync — pre vs post band metrics, ±1 SE. Cross-power and amplitude ratio collapse (well beyond their SEs); coherence stays high and the apparent lag SE balloons (phase no longer stable) — its residual value is meaningless.

Read-off: the key limitation of the native view — WaveletComp’s normalized, smoothed coherence stays near 1 even though the response is noise, and a phase/lag is still numerically returned (with a huge SE). What actually registers the decoupling is the un-normalized cross-power and the band amplitude ratio collapsing to ≈0: the watershed no longer oscillates at the climate period. (Analysis I caught the same event via peak-spacing irregularity — a different fingerprint of the same decoupling.)

Return

Expectation: the excursion peaks then recovers to regime n, so pre and post are the same regime — every band metric should match end-to-end.

plot_resid(d_return, "Return — model residual & its derivative")

Figure 9. Return — model residual (green) + derivative (orange, right axis), whole series; phases shaded, dashed = disturbance. Residual departs during the excursion (derivative spikes at onset and recovery) then settles back to ~0. 

plot_wc(wc_return, "Return — wavelet coherence")

Figure 10. Return — wavelet coherence spectrum. Coupling at the P≈500 band is maintained end-to-end; the transient excursion perturbs phase/power only within the transition window. 

plot_band_trace(tr_return, "Return — synchrony at the P≈500 band, through time")

Figure 11. Return — band metrics through time. A transient wobble during the excursion, then all four metrics return to their pre values by the settled post window. 

emit_table(sm_return, "Return — band synchrony, settled pre vs post (n_blocks = cycles the SEs rest on)")
Return — band synchrony, settled pre vs post (n_blocks = cycles the SEs rest on)
phase n_blocks coherence xpower amp_ratio phi lag
pre 2 1 1.68 0.99 0.62 49.71
post 2 1 1.63 1.00 0.63 49.85 
plot_wave_bars(sm_return, "Return — band metrics, pre vs post (±1 SE)")

Figure 12. Return — pre vs post band metrics, ±1 SE. Every metric matches within error — no lasting signature.

Read-off: pre ≈ post on all four band metrics — the disturbance leaves no lasting wavelet signature, matching analysis I’s null result.

Cross-dataset summary

Post-minus-pre change in the four band metrics, per scenario, each Δ carrying its propagated SE (√(se_pre² + se_post²)) so the change is read against its uncertainty.

sm_all <- bind_rows(Shift = sm_shift, Desync = sm_desync, Return = sm_return, .id = "dataset")

# Long form (metric × value × se) via the shared reshape, then pre→post delta + propagated SE.
delta <- metrics_long(sm_all, c("dataset", "phase")) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = phase, values_from = c(value, se)) %>%
  mutate(delta    = value_post - value_pre,
         delta_se = sqrt(se_pre^2 + se_post^2),
         dataset  = factor(dataset, c("Shift", "Desync", "Return")))
cat(kable(delta %>% transmute(dataset, metric = metric_labs[as.character(metric)],
                              pre = value_pre, post = value_post, delta, delta_se),
          digits = 2, caption = "pre → post change in band metrics, all datasets (delta_se = propagated ±1 SE)"), sep = "\n")
pre → post change in band metrics, all datasets (delta_se = propagated ±1 SE)
dataset metric pre post delta delta_se
Shift coherence 1.00 1.00 0.00 0.00
Shift cross power 2.08 1.03 -1.05 0.06
Shift amp ratio 0.99 0.51 -0.48 0.01
Shift app. lag 49.64 10.67 -38.98 0.38
Desync coherence 1.00 0.99 -0.01 0.01
Desync cross power 1.66 0.07 -1.58 0.02
Desync amp ratio 0.98 0.04 -0.94 0.01
Desync app. lag 50.00 29.39 -20.61 3.18
Return coherence 1.00 1.00 0.00 0.00
Return cross power 1.68 1.63 -0.05 0.06
Return amp ratio 0.99 1.00 0.01 0.01
Return app. lag 49.71 49.85 0.14 0.20 
ggplot(delta, aes(metric, delta, fill = metric)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = delta - delta_se, ymax = delta + delta_se), width = 0.25, colour = "grey25", na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey50") +
  facet_wrap(~ dataset) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = metric_labs) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", guide = "none") +
  labs(title = "Post − pre change by scenario (±1 SE)", x = NULL, y = "post − pre") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

Figure 13. Post − pre change per band metric, faceted by scenario, ±1 propagated SE. Shift = amp-ratio & lag drop (coupled regime shift); Desync = cross-power & amp-ratio collapse (decoupling, invisible to coherence); Return = all Δ ≈ 0.

Takeaways — the native wavelet view, next to analysis I. The Morlet band reading pins the Shift cleanly: amplitude ratio and apparent lag move together (1.0→0.5, 50→10) while coherence holds — the same “coupled regime shift” analysis I inferred, but with a sharp, sub-step lag instead of a few-sample peak offset. Return is a flat null in both views. The instructive disagreement is Desync: the wavelet’s normalized coherence is fooled (stays ≈1, and still hands back a phase/lag), so the decoupling reads only in the un-normalized cross-power and band amplitude collapsing to ≈0 — where analysis I saw it as peak-spacing irregularity. Two methods, two fingerprints of the same event: the lesson is that coherence alone is not enough — you need a power/amplitude channel to see a driver the response has stopped tracking (a significance test wouldn’t rescue it: the saturated coherence would itself read as “significant”). ```
